raw_comment:
This graph depicts the percentage of young people who say they reach out to a parent at least once a day or a few times a week. The graph also shows the means by which participants reach out and the disparity between young women and young men surveyed. I notice that in every category of communication, women reach out to their parent(s) more than men. I wonder why this is. Is it just a matter of inherent psychological differences between men and women?  Additionally, I noticed that visiting a parent in person is the least popular method of reaching out to a parent and texting is the most popular method. I speculate that this is because of convenience. Texting is so easy and can be done in a matter of seconds while driving all the way to visit your parents could take hours depending on how far they live. I am surprised texting and calling are not more popular because they are so accessible and easy to use. I wonder how this demographic would change if they surveyed people older than 34. This relates to me because I am going to college next year and will be forced to reach out to my parents because I will no longer be living with them. I will try to contact them at least a few times per week most frequently using text and calls rather than visits. 
"Message Mothers More!"
ace_sentences: This graph depicts the percentage of young people.
Young people say that they reach out to a parent at least once a day or a few times a week.
The graph shows the means by which participants reach out.
The graph shows the disparity between young women and young men surveyed.
In every category of communication, women reach out to their parent or parents more than men.
I wonder why women reach out to their parent or parents more than men.
It is possible that inherent psychological differences exist between men and women.
Visiting a parent in person is the least popular method of reaching out to a parent.
Texting is the most popular method.
I speculate that texting is the most popular method because of convenience.
Texting is easy.
Texting can be done in a matter of seconds.
Driving all the way to visit your parents could take hours.
How far they live can affect how long driving all the way to visit your parents takes.
I am surprised that texting and calling are not more popular.
Texting and calling are accessible.
Texting and calling are easy to use.
I wonder how this demographic would change if they surveyed people older than 34.
This relates to me.
I am going to college next year.
Next year, I will no longer be living with my parents.
Next year, I will be forced to reach out to my parents.
I will try to contact my parents at least a few times per week.
I will most frequently use text and calls rather than visits.
The message is "Message Mothers More!".
